$comment: Synthetic golden cases only. Historical source details are reduced to drift classes; no customer data or private names are copied.

cases.description: A catalog calls a synthetic node live even though its only resolution failed.

expected_ruling.citation: OPUS.md Part II - catalog/reality assimilation ruling
expected_ruling.rationale: A catalog claim cannot be assimilated as live truth without successful observed resolution.

cases.description: Two synthetic mirrors claim to represent the same contract but differ by one version value.

expected_ruling.citation: OPUS.md Part II - mirror assimilation ruling; ecosystem-spec.json drift_triangle.checks[0]
expected_ruling.rationale: Independent mirrors of one contract must be byte-identical; unequal SHA-256 digests are drift.

cases.description: A synthetic producer emits version 2.0 and two required enum values while its consumer accepts only version 1.0 and one value.

expected_ruling.citation: OPUS.md Part II - schema-version assimilation ruling
expected_ruling.rationale: A producer contract is not assimilated until every required consumer accepts its version and exposes a superset of its required enum.

cases.description: A synthetic rappid suffix is derived from owner/slug instead of stable identity material.

expected_ruling.citation: OPUS.md Part II - identity assimilation ruling, superseding CONSTITUTION Article XXXIV.1
expected_ruling.rationale: sha256(owner/slug) is illegal because location is mutable; the identifier must derive from master_pubkey_SPKI or a stable UUID.

cases.description: A synthetic Bible render remains pinned to 1.0.0 after the machine contract advances to 1.1.0.

expected_ruling.citation: OPUS.md Part II - canonical-pin assimilation ruling; ecosystem-spec.json drift_triangle.checks[2]
expected_ruling.rationale: The human rendering must name the exact machine-contract version it renders.

cases.description: A synthetic distribution intentionally pins an older kernel with a matching digest and passing freeze check.

expected_ruling.citation: OPUS.md Part II - intentional-pin assimilation ruling
expected_ruling.rationale: A version gap is not silent drift when an explicit LTS pin matches the distributed bytes and a freeze check enforces it.

cases.description: Two synthetic records normalize to the same unnamed placeholder.

expected_ruling.citation: OPUS.md Part II - naming assimilation ruling
expected_ruling.rationale: Distinct records cannot be safely assimilated when their public names collapse to one placeholder identity.

cases.description: A synthetic private identifier appears in public text.

fixture.public_text: Deployment notes reference private-synthetic-alpha as an origin.

expected_ruling.rationale: A public artifact must not disclose names from the private side of the estate.

cases.description: Historical corroborated finding represented by a synthetic qualitative catalog claim and a one-time observation.

fixture.catalog_text: The synthetic node operates continuously and autonomously.
fixture.observation_text: One snapshot exists; no refresh cadence or later activity is recorded.
fixture.review_question: Does the one-time synthetic snapshot substantiate the catalog's claim of continuous autonomous operation?

expected_ruling.citation: OPUS.md Part II - catalog/reality assimilation ruling
expected_ruling.rationale: A qualitative operating claim needs evidence at the claimed duration and scope; a one-time snapshot is insufficient.

cases.description: Historical corroborated identity finding represented by a second synthetic owner/slug-derived identifier.

expected_ruling.citation: OPUS.md Part II - identity assimilation ruling, superseding CONSTITUTION Article XXXIV.1
expected_ruling.rationale: An address-derived digest is not a stable identity invariant and must be ruled drift.

cases.description: Historical corroborated privacy finding represented by a synthetic private name in a public index.

expected_ruling.rationale: Private-side names are data even when no contents are exposed; publishing the name is a leak.
